original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in MPLS Bug 199528?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 199528): Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state and triggers a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using a checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: pspo Bug 267398?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: pspo (Bug ID 267398): Symptom: VXLAN tunnels go down after removing interfaces with IPv6 address that are the same as the VXLAN VTEP IP addresses. Scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down. Workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Management Module Bug 279051?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in WebUI Bug 279019?
original_answer: WebUI (Bug ID 279019): Symptom: After uploading an invalid PEM certificate, the hpe-restd process becomes unstable, the WebUI temporarily stops responding, and all REST API calls from the WebUI fail. Scenario: If a user uploads a corrupt PEM certificate file using WebUI certificate management window, selecting the Upload button in the WebUI causes the WebUI to stop working completely. To recover from this state, restart hpe-restd from the bash prompt in the command-line interface or restart the switch. Workaround: Use the CLI to upload certificates.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: Security PA infra Bug 278954?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: Security PA infra (Bug ID 278954): Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 273676?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 273676): Symptom: ERSPAN/Mirror Endpoint interoperability issues occur between platforms with differing ASIC manufacturers. Scenario: There is an established tunnel session between two switches, along with an ERSPAN session using that tunnel. However, the Mirroring Endpoint on the 6xxx/8360 device is not decapsulating the packets. The Mirroring Endpoint expects packets with protocol type 0x6558 for ERSPAN, but the transmitting device is using protocol type 0x88BE. As a result, no packets are being recognized by the Mirror Endpoint. Workaround: Use a SPAN session with the destination of a directly attached device rather than ERSPAN.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 271587?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 271587): Symptom: The NTP conductor won't be available to serve NTP clients on all interfaces for the VRF with both a client and conductor; instead, it will only listen on the configured source interface. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in TPM Bug 265125?
original_answer: TPM (Bug ID 265125): Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in L3 Routes Bug 240831?
original_answer: L3 Routes (Bug ID 240831): Symptom: A route takes 180 seconds to get completely learned and stabilized when the feature enabled using the command route recursive default route is disabled during the route flap. Scenario: A route flap is observed upon any uplink failover scenarios when the nexthop of the BGP route tries to resolve via default route. In order to avoid the flap, route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6 should be disabled. if the cli is disabled during the issue state. Route learning takes 180 seconds for learning it completely. Cli "Route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6" needs to be disabled first. Workaround: clear bgp \*.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 240054?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 240054): Symptom: When DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPS, the DHCP Server will receive duplicate DHCP packets. Scenario: By default, VxLAN tunnel interfaces are trusted. To avoid duplicate packets on DHCP Server when DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPs, should disable trust on VxLAN tunnel interfaces. These configurations can be enabled using the new no dhcpv4-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv4 Snooping and the new no dhcpv6-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv6 Snooping.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in MPLS Bug 199528?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 199528): Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state with a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites learning a total of 40K from each site, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what known issue is documented for Device Profile Bug 288885?
original_answer: Device Profile (Bug ID 288885): Symptom: On a dual port AP, a device profile client failed to onboard on a LAG interface with secure mode enabled. This issue is seen on a device profile with lldp-group matching criteria, but is not observed on a device profile with mac-match matching criteria. Scenrio: This issue occurs when a user connects the Dual AP on some normal physical ports (for example, 1/1/1 and 1/1/2) and makes sure these two ports NOT part of the LAG. LLDP frames exchanged and the output of the show lldp neighbors command shows the entries. Next, configure LAG with AAA and port-access commands to enable security. Add these twp ports to the LAG interface and then observe the Dual AP onboard failure by issuing the command show port-access clients interface lag<lag id>. Workaround: Clear LLDPneighbors and toggle the LAG interface on DUT.

Add these two ports to the LAG interface and then observe the Dual AP onboard failure by issuing the command show port-access clients interface lag<lag id>. Workaround: Clear LLDPneighbors and toggle the LAG interface on DUT.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in Physical Interfaces Bug 224311?
original_answer: Physical Interfaces (Bug ID 224311): Symptom: Smart rate ports do not support half duplex mode. When parallel detection, a function of 10BASE-T and 100BASE-TX auto-negotiation, is enabled, auto-negotiating interfaces that are linked with non auto-negotiating interfaces are expected to link up at the detected speed with half duplex mode. The BASE-T ports on AOS-CX switches have parallel detection enabled and are compliant with the IEEE 802.3 standard. However, there are some interfaces that do not conform with this behavior due to hardware limitations. Scenario: This issue is observed on Aruba CX 6300, 6400, 8360, and 10000 Switch Series for products, JL659A, JL660A, JL720A, JL720C, R0X41A, R0X41C, R0X42A, R0X42C, S1T83A, R9W95A, R9W97A, R8Q71A, R8V12A, R8S89A, R8S90A, R8S91A, S0E91A, and S0X44A. Workaround: It is recommended to enable auto-negotiation on the link partner whenever possible to ensure linking up at the highest speed. Else, users can also explicitly force speed through the speed 100-full command in the interface config context to link with a non-negotiating link partner.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in Physical Interfaces Bug 224311?
cleaned_answer: Physical Interfaces (Bug ID 224311): Symptom: Smart rate ports do not support half duplex mode. When parallel detection is enabled for 10BASE-T and 100BASE-TX auto-negotiation, interfaces linked with non-auto-negotiating interfaces are expected to link up at the detected speed with half duplex mode. The BASE-T ports on AOS-CX switches have parallel detection enabled and comply with the IEEE 802.3 standard. However, some interfaces do not conform due to hardware limitations. Scenario: This issue is observed on Aruba CX 6300, 6400, 8360, and 10000 Switch Series for products JL659A, JL660A, JL720A, JL720C, R0X41A, R0X41C, R0X42A, R0X42C, S1T83A, R9W95A, R9W97A, R8Q71A, R8V12A, R8S89A, R8S90A, R8S91A, S0E91A, and S0X44A. Workaround: Enable auto-negotiation on the link partner whenever possible to ensure linking at the highest speed. Alternatively, explicitly force speed through the speed 100-full command in the interface config context to link with a non-negotiating link partner.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 289568?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 289568): Symptom: The root switch initiates STP re-convergence and causes causing MAC flush and traffic loss. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade process in VSX pair (which acts as non-root switch in RPVST topology). When the primary switch is down, this issue is encountered in a non-root access switch connected to this VSX-pair. Workaround: Install BPDU filter on the root-port of access switch during the software upgrade and remove the filter after the upgrade.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in IGMP/MLD Bug 292078?
original_answer: IGMP/MLD (Bug ID 292078): Symptom: The event message, Event ID 2628: “IGMP/MLD internal queue limit exceeded. Needs admin intervention” is logged multiple times. Scenario: This issue is observed in VXLAN overlay networks that do not have any multicast configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP snooping on each VLAN carried in the overlay on all switches acting as VTEPs. Alternatively, use the logging filter command to deny log messages with the Event ID 2628.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in Central Bug 293314?
original_answer: Central (Bug ID 293314): Symptom: The client list is not populated in the Central client view page. Scenario: In very rare instances, after maintenance is performed on Aruba Central infrastructure, the client list in the Central client view page may fail to list the connected clients for some switches. This issue is uncommon enough that an upgrade to this release may not be warranted if the only reason for the upgrade is to avoid this potential issue. Workaround: From the switch console, enter bash via the start-shell command, and execute the command systemctl restart yang-resolverd.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in VSX Sync Bug 299838?
original_answer: VSX Sync (Bug ID 299838): Symptom: The interface VLAN configuration did not synchronize on the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue is observed in a pair of switches where the user configures an interface VLAN with vsx-sync configuration on the primary VSX and then creates the same interface VLAN on the secondary switch. Workaround: When vsx-sync is enabled on the interface VLAN of the primary VSX member, it is recommended to wait for at least 15 seconds before configuring the same on the secondary switch. Alternatively, configure the interface VLAN on the secondary switch and then enable vsx-sync on the primary switch.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 306746): Symptom: The last_configuration_time is not updated on the switch. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 301734?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 301734): Symptom: The role assigned by CoA gets overridden by low-priority RADIUS assigned roles. Scenario: With default auth-priority set on a port, configure both MAC and 802.1X authentication. After a successful MAC authentication and concurrent onboarding, assign a role from the RADIUS server. While 802.1x is still in the authenticating state, assign a different RADIUS role using CoA and wait till the 802.1X gets deauthenticated. It can be observed that the role assigned by CoA gets overridden by the RADIUS assigned role. Workaround: Remove concurrent onboarding.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in ACL Logging Bug 305843?
original_answer: ACL Logging (Bug ID 305843): Symptom: Switch reloads unexpectedly during a firmware upgrade. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade where a switch might reload while parsing an Access List (ACL) configuration. Workaround: It is recommended to follow the following procedure before the upgrade: Save the full configuration as a checkpoint using the copy running-config checkpoint <name> command. Remove all access-list configurations, save the configuration as the startup configuration using the copy running-config startup-config command. Upgrade the switch. Once the switch is upgraded, move the ACLs back to the running configuration by copying the saved checkpoint to the running configuration using the copy checkpoint <name> running-config command.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in Supportability Bug 311012?
original_answer: Supportability (Bug ID 311012): Symptom: High storage utilization is observed in the file system and as a result, users are unable to copy the support files. Scenario: Logs from various daemons are continuously generated and stored in multiple files in the /fs/logs/boot/ directory. This leads to a high storage utilization and prevents the copying of support files. Workaround: Use the directory mode support for file collection. After upgrading the device to the fixed firmware version, it is necessary to remove the previous boot logs. Performing two system reboots will clear these files.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 298054?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 298054): Symptom: The MAC address learnt on the local VSX switch did not show up on the peer VSX switch. Scenario: This issue occurs when a member port is removed and another member port is added to the ISL lag during the same time. This issue is also observed when a physical port on the ISL lag is changed quickly. Workaround: Delete the existing ISL lag, recreate the LAG, add the ports to the LAG, and then set the LAG as ISL.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Config Mgmt (Bug ID 313068): Symptom: Static routes are deleted after an upgrade from AOS-CX 10.08.1010 to later versions. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what issue was resolved in Central Bug 300693?
original_answer: Central (Bug ID 300693): Symptom: Firmware updates via Aruba Central fails with an event log, Event | 4403 | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | User admin: primary image update failed via HTTPS from common.cloud.hpe.com. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch is configured to use http-proxy and the switch's upstream DNS server does not have an entry for common.cloud.hpe.com and when DNS recursion is disabled. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 312815?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 312815): Symptom: Clients are unable to receive the IP address when DHCP snooping is enabled and when the source MAC address is different from the client hardware address. Scenario: Ethernet MAC address and client hardware address are diffrent for the clients. This issue is observed when DHCPv4 snooping drops the DHCP offer / DHCP acknowledgment packets when the source MAC address differs from the client MAC address field in the DHCP discover / request packets. This issue is seen even when the verify MAC option is disabled. Workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?
original_answer: TFTP (Bug ID 269619): Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what issue was resolved in VSX Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX-Sync is no longer syncing after a command for BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map is used. Scenario: This issue is seen on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration, with syncing for BGP active and when the user configures a redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. After this configuration, VSX-Sync won't sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Either remove the redistribution configuration or disable BGP syncing.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 295438?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 295438): Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?
original_answer: TFTP (Bug ID 269619): Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 305302): Symptom: Performance issues are observed for 1G devices. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 335299?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 335299): Symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 333709?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 333709): Symptom: Interface remains down post a CoA port bounce, with interface details reason showing as Authorization Change. Scenario: On doing a admin shut on the port during the Port-Bounce duration, the interface continues to remain in the down state even after the port-bounce duration. Workaround: Reset the configuration to default by using the default interface <IFNAME> command. This brings the interface back up.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed. This happened after inserting 1G transceiver into 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. this will enable only even number ports for 50G use, the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues observed while inserting 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
cleaned_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed when inserting a 1G transceiver into a 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, grouping in 4 X 25G ports into 2 X 50G ports enables only even-numbered ports for 50G use, while the remaining two odd-numbered ports should be empty. Network issues occur when inserting a 1G transceiver into an empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid combining 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 326400?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 326400): Symptom: Windows virtual machines detect duplicate IP addresses during Windows cluster failover. Scenario: During a Windows cluster failover, a Windows virtual machine sends an ARP probe for its own IP address to detect any duplicate IPs in the network. With ARP suppression enabled, the switch replies to the ARP probe instead of forwarding it through the VXLAN fabric. Workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 326400?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 341465?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 341465): Symptom: Switches are unable to sync with NTP server even though NTP server is reachable and responding to the client. Scenario: Connect to an NTP server that uses special characters for the reference ID field. Workaround: Connect to an NTP server that doesn't uses special characters for the reference ID fields.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 338484?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 338484): Symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted. Scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group. Workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what issue was resolved in MSTP Bug 346938?
original_answer: MSTP (Bug ID 346938): Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?

This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU size limits may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a customer configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to the maximum MTU are incorrectly dropped and are not mirrored. This also applies to remote/tunnel mirrors. Workaround: Configure mirror destination ports as routing port and set the ip mtu size. For a VLAN interface, set the ip mtu on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the ip mtu would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 341536?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 341536): Symptom: Traffic does not flow over MCLAGs, causing the Egress Block to remain unset. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 354044?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 354044): Symptom: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. This results in authentication failures for both 802.1X and MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB) clients. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11. Scenario: When the RADIUS server is configured to return a Session-Timeout attribute with value set to 0 and an 802.1X client attempts to authenticate on the switch, the port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes. Workaround: Remove the Session-Timeout = 0 attribute from the RADIUS server configuration or configure a value greater than 0. Use the client inactivity timeout none in the role configuration instead.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 354044?
cleaned_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 354044): Symptom: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11. Scenario: When the RADIUS server is configured to return a Session-Timeout attribute with value set to 0 and an 802.1X client attempts to authenticate on the switch, the port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes. Workaround: Remove the Session-Timeout = 0 attribute from the RADIUS server configuration or configure a value greater than 0. Use the client inactivity timeout none in the role configuration instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what symptom occurs in Port Access Bug 354044?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 354044): The symptom is: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. This results in authentication failures for both 802.1X and MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB) clients. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what is the workaround for Port Access Bug 354044?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. This causes the higher local preference route to be selected.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 359279?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 359279): Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 359666?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 338204?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time and delays the channel change, which leads to the forwarding port change in the hardware. Workaround: The multicast activity-status update is improved to fetch the status from all of the LCs at the same time instead of fetching it one-by-one from each of the LCs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 342612?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 342612): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. The higher local preference route is selected.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal:6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in MSTP Bug 348612?
original_answer: MSTP (Bug ID 348612): Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 364576?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 364576): Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. Scenario: This issue may occur in an EVPN VXLAN environment with VSX pairs when a VIP (Virtual IP) moves from one node to another node, MAC address aging occurs or after executing "clear mac-address" on the switch. Workaround: A temporary workaround is to add a static ARP entry for the affected IP addresses.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in Physical Interfaces Bug 373334?
original_answer: Physical Interfaces (Bug ID 373334): Symptom: Switch-Transceiver is in down state with reason "Module interface error.". Scenario: This issue occurs when flow control is enabled on a particular interface and bi-directional traffic is sent at line rate of 99%. When a shut and no shut (link up down) is done at a particular interface module, interface error is seen. Workaround: Call pv_network_ports_enable(node_id, _port, False) after disabling TM using pv_tm_port_disable.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in L3 routes Bug 375775?
original_answer: L3 routes (Bug ID 375775): Symptom: Traffic loss in an SVI with vsx active-forwarding enabled, when vsx active-forwarding is disabled/removed on a entirely different SVI. Scenario: In a configuration where vsx active-forwarding is enabled, a special HW resource must be programmed for the VSX peers to handle traffic destined to the active-gateway IP. The trigger for this programming is tied to the router MAC programming), and the ref counting for these router MACs would decrement early, removing the special HW resource and causing traffic loss to occur depending on which peer it lands on. Workaround: There are two possible workarounds: 1. Unconfigure and reconfigure vsx active-forwarding in the SVI. 2. Save the configuration and reboot the VSX peers.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what issue was resolved in PVST Bug 389395?
original_answer: PVST (Bug ID 389395): Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what issue was resolved in Slot Management Bug 384894?
original_answer: Slot Management (Bug ID 384894): Symptom: Two network switches each contain a port with identical MAC addresses. Scenario: Two switches in the same network have adjacent base MAC addresses. Because of a flaw in the way that port MAC addresses are assigned in the system, Switch #1's port 1/1/2 is assigned an identical MAC address as Switch #2's base MAC address. This MAC address overlap within a network can cause automation processes to incorrectly identify both devices as the same entity, leading to operational issues. Workaround: Ensure that no two switches in a network have consecutive base MAC addresses, or do not use both of the ports with the overlap.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what known issue is documented for L3 routes Bug 385909?
original_answer: L3 routes (Bug ID 385909): Symptom: Support files collection hangs while trying to get vs-appctl -t hpe-routing hpe-metaswitch/show_ip_rib information. Scenario: While DCMD initiates the support files collection, the hpe-routing appctl command launches from vtysh and has been running in the background. Since the IDL connection gets inherited, the ovsdb-server treats the IDL connection as active established state and tries to push IDL updates intended for vtysh, which is non-existent in the system. In the VSX environment, the IDL updates push over the ISL, and spikes up the systemd-socket-proxyd daemon to 100% as there are no vtysh active in the system to process these updates.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what known issue is documented for L3 routes Bug 385909?
cleaned_answer: L3 routes (Bug ID 385909): Symptom: Support files collection hangs while trying to get vs-appctl -t hpe-routing hpe-metaswitch/show_ip_rib information. Scenario: While DCMD initiates the support files collection, the hpe-routing appctl command launches from vtysh and has been running in the background. Since the IDL connection gets inherited, the ovsdb-server treats the IDL connection as active established state and tries to push IDL updates intended for vtysh, which is non-existent in the system. In the VSX environment, the IDL updates push over the ISL, and spikes up the systemd-socket-proxyd daemon to 100% as there are no vtysh active in the system to process these updates.
