original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what issue was resolved in Container Manager Bug 366132?
original_answer: Container Manager (Bug ID 366132): Symptom: Container statuses are not updated after deleting and recreating the containers. Scenario: This issue can occur with these following time sensitive steps: 1. Container A is created, Container Manager is processing the creation of said container. 2. A delete request for container A is sent using CLI or REST. 3. A request to create container A is sent. 4. When checking for statuses using REST or show container, the container is marked as down. Workaround: Where possible, avoid deleting and recreating the same container while it is being created, allow it to reach the operational state. If the issue is encountered, delete the container using REST or CLI and restart the switch to release the occupied container resources.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Central Connectivity (Bug ID 317632): Symptom: AOS-CX switches are reaching out to Google Public DNS server: 8.8.8.8. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 277126?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): Symptom: Classifier or PBR Policy is not enforced for traffic coming via ISL link. Scenario: In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting the classifier policy enforced. Workaround: To prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL, use active gateway instead of VRRP. This provides better active-active redundancy and packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to active gateway.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 400513?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 400513): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in OSPFv2 Bug 413286?
original_answer: OSPFv2 (Bug ID 413286): Symptom: OSPF default route in area 0 points to a stub router instead of ASBR via backbone area. Scenario: This issue can occur when there are two paths to the ASBR that generated the external route - one via stub area and another via non-stub area. The external route should get installed with the path reachable through the non-stub area, but the path via stub area gets installed, leading to a forwarding loop. Workaround: Enable rfc1583_compatability under the router ospf context.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 215677?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 215677): Symptom: IGMP/MLD configuration changes do not take effect when the hpe-mgmdd process is flooded with IGMP/MLD control packets. Scenario: In a situation where IGMP or MLD control packets (Joins or Leaves) are part of a loop, or if misbehaving clients send continuous streams of IGMP/MLD control packets, the hpe-mgmdd daemon's packet queue can become continuously full. Workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

reason: The original answer contains unclear wording and lacks consistency in describing the issue and workaround. It also includes specific port numbers which may not be relevant for all users.
original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in Physical Interfaces Bug 224311?
original_answer: Physical Interfaces (Bug ID 224311): Symptom: When parallel detection is enabled, autonegotiating interfaces that are linked with non-autonegotiating interfaces are expected link up at the detected speed, but in half duplex mode. BASE-T ports on AOS-CX switches have parallel detection enabled and are compliant the IEEE 802.3 standard. However, there are some interfaces that are not conforming to this behavior due to hardware limitations. Scenario: The following ports have non-conforming parallel detection, or does not have parallel detection enabled. JL659A: Ports 1-48, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex JL660A: Ports 1-24, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex JL720A, JL720C: Ports 11-48, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex R0X41A, R0X41C: Ports 1-48, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex R0X42A, R0X42C, S1T83A: Ports 1-24, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex R9W95A: Ports 1-24, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex R9W97A: Ports 11-40, Enabled for 100M but links at full duplex R8Q71A, R8V12A: Ports 37-48, Not enabled (will not link with non-autonegotiating partner) R8S89A: Ports 1-24, Not enabled (will not link with non-autonegotiating partner) R8S90A, R8S91A: Ports 11-48, Not enabled (will not link with non-autonegotiating partner) S0E91A, S0X44A: Ports 1-48, Not enabled (will not link with non-autonegotiating partner). Workaround: Enable autonegotiation on the link partner whenever possible to ensure linking up at highest speed. If not possible, you can also explicitly force speed through the speed 100-full command in the interface-config context to link up with a non-negotiating link partner (assuming the link partner is 100M full duplex).

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in LLDP Bug 287305?
original_answer: LLDP (Bug ID 287305): Symptom: A Client is assigned a role VLAN instead of a Port VLAN ID (PVID). Scenario: This issue occurs on a Device-profile client onboarded with LLDP neighbor info, whenever the user disconnects AP, which is already assigned DP profile, then unplugs the AP and connects the Notebook/Workstation. It received the IP address from the device profile native role assigned VLAN. HOwever, It is supposed to receive the VLAN IP address from the PVID VLAN. Workaround: Wait for the LLDP neighbor entry to age out for the device that was unplugged before plugging in a different device to the same port.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 299838?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 299838): Symptom: An Interface VLAN configuration is not getting synced to secondary switch. Scenario: This issue can occur n a pair of switches in VSX configuration, when the user creates an Interface VLAN with VSX-Sync configuration in primary switch, then the user creates the same Interface VLAN on secondary, but the configuration is not synced. Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary interface. Another workaround is to first create the Interface VLAN on the secondary and then enable VSX-Sync on the primary Interface VLAN. (There is no need to wait in this second workaround).
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 299838?
cleaned_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 299838): Symptom: An Interface VLAN configuration is not getting synced to the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue can occur in a pair of switches in VSX configuration, when the user creates an Interface VLAN with VSX-Sync configuration on the primary switch and then creates the same Interface VLAN on the secondary switch, but the configuration is not synced. Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on the primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary switch. Another workaround is to first create the Interface VLAN on the secondary switch and then enable VSX-Sync on the primary Interface VLAN. (There is no need to wait in this second workaround).

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 299851?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 299851): Symptom: The VSX software upgrade software version is not properly validated when upgrading using TFTP. Scenario: When upgrading using TFTP, it is possible to load mismatched software versions, resulting in a VSX upgrade failure and a VSX software mismatch between the primary and secondary VSX nodes. Workaround: Confirm that software image versions are compatible. Software upgrades using boot banks rather than TFTP will perform a proper version check before completing the upgrade.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 300754?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 300754): Symptom: An attempt to learn a number of MAC addresses on the switch equal to the capacity of the MAC Table could trigger a Critical Service Fault. Scenario: This issue is triggered by connecting an extremely large number of devices or running test scripts/tools that generate traffic with a large number of Source MACs. Workaround: Limit the number of Source MAC addresses in the environment to 90% of the MAC table Capacity.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: Security PA infra Bug 301734?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: Security PA infra (Bug ID 301734): Symptom: A role assigned by CoA gets overridden by low-priority methods, such as a RADIUS assigned role. Scenario: This issue occurs on a switch with the default auth-priority on port ifyou configure both mac-auth and dot1x and assign a role from RADIUS on successful mac-auth authentication and concurrent on boarding. While dot1x is in authenticating state, if you assign a different role RADIUS using CoA, you can observe that role assigned by CoA gets overridden by the RADIUS-assigned role (during mac-auth success). Workaround: Remove concurrent onboarding.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: Security PA infra Bug 301734?
cleaned_answer: Internal srvcs: Security PA infra (Bug ID 301734): Symptom: A role assigned by CoA gets overridden by low-priority methods, such as a RADIUS assigned role. Scenario: This issue occurs on a switch with the default auth-priority on port if you configure both mac-auth and dot1x and assign a role from RADIUS on successful mac-auth authentication and concurrent onboarding. While dot1x is in authenticating state, if you assign a different role via RADIUS using CoA, you can observe that the role assigned by CoA gets overridden by the RADIUS-assigned role (during mac-auth success). Workaround: Remove concurrent onboarding.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in RADsec Bug 308854?
original_answer: RADsec (Bug ID 308854): Symptom: Client authentication failures can occur, and packets sent to a RADIUS server are timed out by the switch. Scenario: In some scenarios when the RADSec server connection is lost and restored while multiple port-access users are authenticated to the RADIUS server the switch might incorrectly time out the RADIUS packets. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?
original_answer: TFTP (Bug ID 269619): Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what known issue is documented for Audio-Video Bridging Bug 312492?
original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 313098?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 313098): Symptom: The show bgp ipv4 unicast command output can show an EVPN route while the output of the show ip route command indicates that it has anECMP route. Senario: When the same prefix is learned from two address families on on BGP (for example from BGP IPv4 unicast with a lesser AS-PATH length and from L2VPN EVPN address-family with a higher AS-PATH length) BGP RIB would pick the EVPN route as the best.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what known issue is documented for Audio-Video Bridging Bug 312492?
original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 295438?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 295438): Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?

original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: The RPVST daemon crashes unexpectedly. Scenario: This issue is observed when an STP-aware switch is connected to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?
original_answer: TFTP (Bug ID 269619): Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what known issue is documented for Audio-Video Bridging Bug 312492?
original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 333709?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 333709): Symptom: Interface remains down post a CoA port bounce, with interface details reason showing as Authorization Change. Scenario: On doing a admin shut on the port during the Port-Bounce duration, the interface continues to remain in the down state even after the port-bounce duration. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed. This happened after inserting 1G transceiver into 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. this will enable only even number ports for 50G use, the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues observed while inserting 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 341465?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 341465): Symptom: Switch is unable to sync with NTP server even though NTP server is reachable and responding to the client. Scenario: In some scenarios when connecting to a NTP server that uses special characters for the reference ID field. The NTP client device will not be able to sync with the server and the error Delimiter for jitter error will be displayed. Workaround: Connect to an NTP server that doesn't uses special characters for the reference ID fields.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 338484?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 338484): Symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted. Scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group. Workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what issue was resolved in MSTP Bug 346938?
original_answer: MSTP (Bug ID 346938): Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. Scenario: When 3rd party legacy devices send packets with source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 to CX devices intermittent BPDU starvation might be observed. Workaround: Configure ACL to drop invalid source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 in all switches where end devices are connected.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU size limits may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a customer configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to the maximum MTU are incorrectly dropped and are not mirrored. This also applies to remote/tunnel mirrors. Workaround: Configure mirror destination ports as routing port and set the ip mtu size. For a VLAN interface, set the ip mtu on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the ip mtu would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 354044?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 354044): Symptom: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. This results in authentication failures for both 802.1X and MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB) clients. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11. Scenario: When the RADIUS server is configured to return a Session-Timeout attribute with value set to 0 and an 802.1X client attempts to authenticate on the switch, the port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes. Workaround: Remove the Session-Timeout = 0 attribute from the RADIUS server configuration or configure a value greater than 0. Use the client inactivity timeout none in the role configuration instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 359279?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 359279): Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 359666?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 342612?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 342612): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

original_answer: IPFIX (Bug ID 357871): Symptom: In Aruba Central Network (CNX), the application total usage statistics displayed for a wired client are significantly higher than the interface-level total usage statistics for the same client. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in VLANs Bug 348612?
original_answer: VLANs (Bug ID 348612): Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode as follows: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in AAA Bug 322898?
original_answer: AAA (Bug ID 322898): Symptom: A client fails to get access to the network after successful authentication. Scenario: This issue can impact a client trying to onboard with a downloadable role after temporary network issues. Workaround: Log off all the clients that are with the Downloadable User Role (DUR) in the failed state using command port-access log-off client role <role-name>. This will initiate a retry of download of the role.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Activate Bug 317632?
original_answer: Activate (Bug ID 317632): Symptom: The switch contacts the Google Public DNS server: 8.8.8.8. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Active Gateway Bug 319555?
original_answer: Active Gateway (Bug ID 319555): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: Configuring the same MAC address for both VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC can cause internal potential network issues. Scenario: Configuring the same MAC address for VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC leads to conflicts. The system-mac VALUE, VALUE-1, and VALUE+1 are used for VSX internal processes, particularly in relation to spanning-tree. Any MAC address collision with system-mac may have adverse side effects. Workaround: Ensure that the active-gateway MAC address is different from the VSX system-mac address. Take care to avoid reusing the system-mac address for the active-gateway.

The system-mac VALUE, VALUE-1, and VALUE+1 are used for VSX internal processes, particularly in relation to spanning-tree. Any MAC address collision with system-mac may have adverse side effects.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in BFD Bug 322265?
original_answer: BFD (Bug ID 322265): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: The copy <remote-url> startup-config command is not working. Scenario: The copy process initiated using the copy <remote-url> startup-config command is not working when IPv6 OSPF Bi-directional Forwarding Detection (BFD) is configured to the same link local address using the REST API or command-line interface. Workaround: Back up a configuration in JSON format or configure BFD to the same link-local address only once.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 325316?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 325316): Symptom: A critical log message is generated for a ipsavd process daemon restart with signal 11. Scenario: This daemon restart will be observed when a DHCP client sends a DHCP renew packet without the end option when dhcpv4-snooping option 82 is disabled. Workaround: Enable dhcpv4-snooping option 82, or configure a maximum lease-time for the client (365 days) to avoid this issue.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in EVPN/BGP Bug 307091?
original_answer: EVPN/BGP (Bug ID 307091): Symptom: The EVPN route selection process may cause an suboptimal path selected. Scenario: During the EVPN best path selection, the IGP metric to reach the nexthop of the EVPN route may not be taken into the consideration. Workaround: Other BGP attributes (like Local preference or weight, etc.) need to be altered for the routes to select the desired path. However this is may not be feasible in all the routing policy/scenarios for the enterprise.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in MPLS Bug 314142?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 314142): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: An MPLS Entry binding entered the configuration_error state, and MPLS forwarding failed. Scenario: This issue occurs when configuring an ROP port as MPLS, then rapidly tearing down all MPLS configurations and reconfiguring it as an SVI. Workaround: Log in to the bash shell, and restart hpe-routing daemon using the command sudo systemctl restart hpe-routing.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 305302?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 305302): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: Low performance speed is observed on 1G NIC cards. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: A 50G interface is not coming up and network reachability issues are observed after inserting a 1G transceiver into an interface in 50G mode on a 8360 Switch. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, changing a group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports will enable only even number ports for 50G use, so the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues were observed while inserting a 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

reason: The original answer includes a scenario that is not relevant to the bug ID mentioned in the question (Bug ID 312081). The cleaned answer should focus on the specific issue resolved by Bug ID 312081.
original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 312081?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 312081): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: A wireless client roaming from a VSX primary to a VSX secondary does not get access to the network until one iteration of the MAC ageout configuration (which by default is 300 seconds). Scenario: This issue occurs when moving a wireless client from one AP in a VSX primary to another AP in a VSX secondary. Both the AP-connected ports in the switch will be operating in device mode. After the client moves, traffic gets dropped as the switch doesn't update the MAC entries until the MAC ageout time. Workaround: Disable port-access secure client-move using the command no port-access client-move enable secure.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Port Security Bug 243450?
original_answer: Port Security (Bug ID 243450): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: The port-access log-off command is not removing port-security sticky clients when the port is down or in an error-disabled state. Scenario: This issue can impact dynamically learned port-security sticky clients when using the port-access log-off client mac command. Additionally, this command cannot be issued when the interface is unavailable; it must be operational or it leads to the client not being deleted from the interface. Workaround: Physically bring interface down by pulling out the cable.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in PTP Bug 321437?
original_answer: PTP (Bug ID 321437): (for 8100 Switch series only) Symptom: The switch will lose synchronization with the Grand-source clock. The offset and path-delay in the output of the show ptp clock command will remain frozen and occasionally a diagnostic dump for the PTP daemon created using the diag-dump command will also timeout. Scenario: The issue is seen when the system is running for more than a day with several PTP clients (20+ devices) connected to the switch directly. With more clients, the activity of the PTP daemon is increased and so the internal logging activity. At some point, there is a hourly automatic log-rotation triggered by log-mgmtd and if the PTP log ( /var/log/ptp.log) filesize has reached to a larger value (more than 500mb) then there is high chance this issue may occur. Immediately after the rotation, the PTP daemon might hang and network administrators may observe the described symptoms. Workaround: Use the following workarounds to reduce the intensity of the logging or stopping the logging all together for PTP daemon. Workaround one: Use this workaround to reduce logging to critical errors only. Use the following procedures after a system reboot, a PTP daemon restart or any PTP global event like enabling or disabling PTP, or a change to PTP clock-step, transport or ptp modes. 1. Issue the systemctl stop ptpd command to stop the ptpd daemon. 2. Issue the rm /var/log/ptp.log command to remove /var/log/ptp.log. 3. Issue the systemctl start ptpd command to start the ptpd daemon. 4. Log in to the command-line interface and issue the show ptp clock command to verify that ptp is running and that the clock offset has a non-zero value and is updating continuously 5. From the bash prompt, execute the following commands: Copy ovs-appctl -t ptpd ptp/ms_set_trace_level 28 0.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for RADIUS Port-Access Bug 320342?
original_answer: RADIUS Port-Access (Bug ID 320342): Symptom: The RADIUS server hostname will get updated to the renewed IP addresses found in DNS server. Scenario: The problem is seen when the host addresses in DNS servers get updated against the hostnames, but the Switch fails to refresh its cache with the renewed address. Workaround: The default refresh timer configured in the switch is 300 seconds. A network administrator can configure a custom value according to their needs after which the switch would automatically update the host addresses of the RADIUS server in its cache.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what caveat is documented for VxLAN-Underlay-Mcast-Replication?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: VxLAN-Underlay-Mcast-Replication - Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a 3-clos data center design when there is link failure between the VTEP and the spine, the underlay multicast routing path towards the RP picks up a leaf VTEP as a transit router, which can cause traffic loss.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in AAA Bug 322898?
original_answer: AAA (Bug ID 322898): Symptom: A client fails to get access to the network after successful authentication. Scenario: This issue can impact a client trying to onboard with a downloadable role after temporary network issues. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Active Gateway Bug 319555?
original_answer: Active Gateway (Bug ID 319555): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: Configuring the same MAC address for both VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC can cause internal potential network issues. Scenario: Configuring the same MAC address for VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC leads to conflicts. The system-mac VALUE, VALUE-1, and VALUE+1 are used for VSX internal processes, particularly in relation to spanning-tree. Any MAC address collision with system-mac may have adverse side effects. Workaround: Ensure that the active-gateway MAC address is different from the VSX system-mac address. Take care to avoid reusing the system-mac address for the active-gateway.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in BFD Bug 322265?
original_answer: BFD (Bug ID 322265): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: The copy <remote-url> startup-config command is not working. Scenario: The copy process initiated using the copy <remote-url> startup-config command is not working when IPv6 OSPF Bi-directional Forwarding Detection (BFD) is configured to the same link local address using the REST API or command-line interface. Workaround: Back up a configuration in JSON format or configure BFD to the same link-local address only once.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 325316?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 325316): Symptom: A critical log message is generated for a ipsavd process daemon restart with signal 11. Scenario: This daemon restart will be observed when a DHCP client sends a DHCP renew packet without the end option when dhcpv4-snooping option 82 is disabled. Workaround: Enable dhcpv4-snooping option 82, or configure a maximum lease-time for the client (365 days) to avoid this issue.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in EVPN/BGP Bug 307091?
original_answer: EVPN/BGP (Bug ID 307091): Symptom: The EVPN route selection process may cause an suboptimal path selected. Scenario: During the EVPN best path selection, the IGP metric to reach the nexthop of the EVPN route may not be taken into the consideration. Workaround: Other BGP attributes (like Local preference or weight, etc.) need to be altered for the routes to select the desired path. However this is may not be feasible in all the routing policy/scenarios for the enterprise.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in MPLS Bug 314142?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 314142): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: An MPLS Entry binding entered the configuration_error state, and MPLS forwarding failed. Scenario: This issue occurs when configuring an ROP port as MPLS, then rapidly tearing down all MPLS configurations and reconfiguring it as an SVI. Workaround: Log in to the bash shell, and restart hpe-routing daemon using the command sudo systemctl restart hpe-routing.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 305302?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 305302): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: Low performance speed is observed on 1G NIC cards. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
original_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: A 50G interface is not coming up and network reachability issues are observed after inserting a 1G transceiver into an interface in 50G mode on a 8360 Switch. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, changing a group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports will enable only even number ports for 50G use, so the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues were observed while inserting a 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?
cleaned_answer: Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Symptom: A 50G interface is not coming up and network reachability issues are observed after inserting a 1G transceiver into an interface in 50G mode on an 8360 Switch. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, changing a group of 4 X 25G ports to 2 X 50G ports enables only even-numbered ports for 50G use, so the remaining two odd-numbered ports should be empty. Network issues were observed while inserting a 1G transceiver into an empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in PIM-SM Bug 328568?

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 312081?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 312081): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: A wireless client roaming from a VSX primary to a VSX secondary does not get access to the network until one iteration of the MAC ageout configuration (which by default is 300 seconds). Scenario: This issue occurs when moving a wireless client from one AP in a VSX primary to another AP in a VSX secondary. Both the AP-connected ports in the switch will be operating in device mode. After the client moves, traffic gets dropped as the switch doesn't update the MAC entries until the MAC ageout time. Workaround: Disable port-access secure client-move using the command no port-access client-move enable secure.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Port Security Bug 243450?
original_answer: Port Security (Bug ID 243450): (For 8360 Switch Series only) Symptom: The port-access log-off command is not removing port-security sticky clients when the port is down or in an error-disabled state. Scenario: This issue can impact dynamically learned port-security sticky clients when using the port-access log-off client mac command. Additionally, this command cannot be issued when the interface is unavailable; it must be operational or it leads to the client not being deleted from the interface. Workaround: Physically bring interface down by pulling out the cable.

reason: The answer contains detailed technical information about a bug and its resolution, which may require review to ensure accuracy and clarity.
original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in PTP Bug 321437?
original_answer: PTP (Bug ID 321437): (for 8100 Switch series only) Symptom: The switch will lose synchronization with the Grand-source clock. The offset and path-delay in the output of the show ptp clock command will remain frozen and occasionally a diagnostic dump for the PTP daemon created using the diag-dump command will also timeout. Scenario: The issue is seen when the system is running for more than a day with several PTP clients (20+ devices) connected to the switch directly. With more clients, the activity of the PTP daemon is increased and so the internal logging activity. At some point, there is a hourly automatic log-rotation triggered by log-mgmtd and if the PTP log ( /var/log/ptp.log) filesize has reached to a larger value (more than 500mb) then there is high chance this issue may occur. Immediately after the rotation, the PTP daemon might hang and network administrators may observe the described symptoms. Workaround: Use the following workarounds to reduce the intensity of the logging or stopping the logging all together for PTP daemon. Workaround one: Use this workaround to reduce logging to critical errors only. Use the following procedures after a system reboot, a PTP daemon restart or any PTP global event like enabling or disabling PTP, or a change to PTP clock-step, transport or ptp modes. 1. Issue the systemctl stop ptpd command to stop the ptpd daemon. 2. Issue the rm /var/log/ptp.log command to remove /var/log/ptp.log. 3. Issue the systemctl start ptpd command to start the ptpd daemon. 4. Log in to the command-line interface and issue the show ptp clock command to verify that ptp is running and that the clock offset has a non-zero value and is updating continuously 5. From the bash prompt, execute the following commands: Copy ovs-appctl -t ptpd ptp/ms_set_trace_level 28 0.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for TFTP Bug 269619?
original_answer: TFTP (Bug ID 269619): Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for Audio-Video Bridging Bug 312492?
original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for RADIUS Port-Access Bug 320342?
original_answer: RADIUS Port-Access (Bug ID 320342): Symptom: The RADIUS server hostname will get updated to the renewed IP addresses found in DNS server. Scenario: The problem is seen when the host addresses in DNS servers get updated against the hostnames, but the Switch fails to refresh its cache with the renewed address. Workaround: The default refresh timer configured in the switch is 300 seconds. A network administrator can configure a custom value according to their needs after which the switch would automatically update the host addresses of the RADIUS server in its cache.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_answer: MAC Table (Bug ID 340999): Symptom: The switch doesnt learn the MAC of a client that is denied on a secure port even when its moved to a trusted/non-secure port. Scenario: Configure port-access on a port (MAC_Auth/Dot1x/port-security). Setup the client MAC that is trying to onboard such that the MAC is denied. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 339717): Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGS, a ops-switchd crash might be observed. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the lag.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?
cleaned_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 339717): Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or support files. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLANs on those LAGs. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGs might cause an ops-switchd crash. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring VLANs for the LAG.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 335246?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 335246): Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: ACL fails to apply only the VSX secondary switch. Scenario: In some scenarios when a config checkpoint restore or classifier list-update is performed in a VSX member, the ACL config will not be applied to the VSX secondary if the ACL config has vsx-sync is enabled. Workaround: Remove the ACL configuration and then re-apply the ACL.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for Audio-Video Bridging Bug 312492?
original_answer: Audio-Video Bridging (Bug ID 312492): Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for MPLS Bug 194381?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 194381): Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for DHCP Bug 405873?
original_answer: DHCP (Bug ID 405873): Symptom: When modifying DHCP Server pool parameters (such as DNS server entries) using Aruba Central template-based configuration, the changes are accepted without warning but do not take effect. The running DHCP configuration continues using the previous values. Scenario: Configuration updates to a DHCP Server pool require the DHCP Server to be disabled and re-enabled (or the switch rebooted) before the new settings become active. When these same changes are pushed through Central templates, they do not take effect. Workaround: For CX software versions prior to 10.15.1020, after making any DHCP server pool configuration change, perform one of the following to activate the new configuration Disable and re-enable the DHCP server instance, or Restart the DHCP server process, or Reboot the switch.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 341536?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 341536): Symptom: Traffic does not flow over MCLAGs, causing the Egress Block to remain unset. This results in traffic from the ISL being sent out over the MCLAG, potentially causing connected devices to receive duplicate packets. Scenario: When configuring an MCAG with AFC, traffic does not flow over MCLAGs. Workaround: Create a new VLAN on the devices where the MCLAG was configured.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU size limits may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a customer configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to the maximum MTU are incorrectly dropped and are not mirrored. This also applies to remote/tunnel mirrors. Workaround: Configure mirror destination ports as routing port and set the ip mtu size. For a VLAN interface, set the ip mtu on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the ip mtu would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 354044?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 354044): Symptom: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. This results in authentication failures for both 802.1X and MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB) clients. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11. Scenario: When the RADIUS server is configured to return a Session-Timeout attribute with value set to 0 and an 802.1X client attempts to authenticate on the switch, the port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes. Workaround: Remove the Session-Timeout = 0 attribute from the RADIUS server configuration or configure a value greater than 0. Use the client inactivity timeout none in the role configuration instead.

original_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. This causes the higher local preference route to be selected.
cleaned_question: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?

Scenario: In some scenarios, BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path, leading to incorrect paths being propagated to the RIB and FIB, which can cause routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated routers. This causes the higher local preference route to be selected.
